  • Reference

Simulate D/L accumulation over a full temperature history

Source: R/kinetics.R
racemize.Rd

Integrates racemization from the oldest to the youngest point in a temperature time series, splitting each timestep into summer and winter halves. Returns the final D/L ratio after back-transforming from \((D/L)^x\) space.

Usage

racemize(age, sumT, winT, x, dT, startingDL = 0, scenario = NA, ...)

Arguments

age

Numeric vector of ages (ka BP). Sorted old-to-young internally.

sumT

Numeric vector of summer half-year temperatures (°C), same length as age.

winT

Numeric vector of winter half-year temperatures (°C), same length as age.

x

Power-law exponent. Default 3.

dT

Timestep in years.

startingDL

Starting D/L value. Default 0 (unracemized).

scenario

Character label stored in the output. Default NA.

...

Additional arguments passed to arrhenius.

Value

A one-row data frame with columns age (maximum input age), DL, and scenario.

See also

racemizeSample for a version that also applies sediment temperature damping; dampSedTemps for the damping model.

Examples

age  <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.01)
sumT <- rep(15, length(age))
winT <- rep(4,  length(age))
racemize(age, sumT, winT, x = 3, dT = 10)
#>   age        DL scenario
#> 1  10 0.2383851       NA
